keys: Hybrid, Half-Elf, Half-Orc, Half-Dwarf, Demihuman, Half-Beast, Half-Sea
value:
A Hybrid is any individual born from two different races.
Hybrids share traits from both parents, often blending physical features, senses, or minor abilities.
Due to how chromosomes function in this world, quarter‑bloods do not exist.
A child of a Hybrid and a pure‑blood parent becomes either:
-a Hybrid (same two‑race combination), or
- a pure‑blood of the non‑Hybrid parent’s race
- No further fractional dilution occurs.
title: Hybrid

value:

It is saturated with raw magical energy, gold‑aligned mana, and draconic vitality, making it one of the most potent alchemical substances in existence.
Its energizing nature allows it to store, amplify, and stabilize magical properties inside potions — a function no other ingredient can replicate.
Required for Elixir.
title: True Gold Dragon Blood

value:
Sacred Site located one one of the worlds mana veins. It purifies all miasma in the sorrounding and makes the land naturally lush and prosperous.
Coveted by most nations, a nation with a Sacred Site is usually defined by the site itself.
Tyrants cannot directly attack the sacred site and the sorrounding area making it a safe zone (extreme weakening).
It's possible to confirm the safety of the hero tied to the sacred site, there are more than one Sacred Site for the same virtue spread all over the world.
Related to Vita Aspect
Managed by a spirit related to the virtue of the site:
- Temperance: Ryu
- Chastity: Unicorn
- Charity: Dog
- Diligence: Beaver
- Patience: Tortoise
- Kindness: Deer
- Modesty: Dove
title: Sacred Site

value:
An Elemental is a magical creature composed entirely of a single elemental force.
Its body is not flesh or bone, but a living mass of ice, fire, water, wind, lightning, or earth, depending on the Aspect that summoned it.

Elementals are manifestations of raw elemental energy given form and purpose.

Lesser Elementals
- amorphous blobs of pure element
- simple instincts
- limited mobility
- used as basic summons or workers

Greater Elementals
- humanoid or semi‑humanoid forms
- defined limbs and features
- higher intelligence
- capable of complex combat and tasks
title: Elemental

value:
A Death Knight is an undead warrior whose sanity is anchored to a binding oath.
This oath is usually one of servitude — most commonly to a Lich, Lich Lord, or Necromancer — but can also be tied to a personal vow, a fallen kingdom, or a sworn duty.
Without this oath, a Death Knight would lose its mind to miasma corruption and become a mindless killing machine.
title: Death Knight

value:
A Lich is a former magic user or necromancer who has merged their soul with miasma, achieving a form of undeath that preserves memory, magical ability, and partial sanity.
Unlike lesser undead, a Lich is not animated by miasma — it feeds on it, controls it, and is empowered by its presence.
Liches are the midpoint between mortal necromancers and the far rarer Lich Lords.
title: Lich

value:
Necromancer is not a class, it refers to a person who learned the taboo art of using miasma without purification.
Unlike demons who refine miasma into mana, necromancers use miasma directly.
title: Necromancer

value:
Lich Lord is an undead former mortal who mastered and become one with Miasma and retained intellingence.
The first Lich Lord was a greedy king who with his court wizards who become the first Necromancers weaponized miasma.
The name of the country and name of the king was erased from the history refered only as the Necropolis.
title: Lich Lord

value:
Sludge
- A slime made of miasma
- Tries to corrode anything without miasma
- Attracted to entities without miasma
title: Sludge

value:
Zombies
- Form when miasma animates a corpse, will eventually rot away and become a skeleton
- Instinctively tries to spread more miasma and kill the living
- Driven by lingering instinct or emotion
title: Zombie

value:
Corrupted Plantlife (Treants)
- Plants absorb ambient miasma
- Become twisted, aggressive, and semi‑sentient
- Forests with high miasma become cursed zones
- Produce more Miasma
title: Treant

value:
Ghosts
- Form when miasma condenses without a body
- Manifest from strong regrets or unfinished business, instinctively tries to spread more miasma and kill the living
- Immune to physical attacks but can still be killed with a strong punch due to vitality of a persons body
title: Ghost

value:
Skeletons
- Form when miasma animates a corpse, may start from a zombie but rots away eventually
- Instinctively tries to spread more miasma and kill the living
- Driven by lingering instinct or emotion
title: Skeleton

value:
Miasma is the spiritual pollution produced by negative emotions, curses, and unresolved grudges.
It is the residue of suffering — the emotional “smoke” left behind when life ends in fear, anger, regret, or despair.
All living beings produce miasma when they die, even plants.
The stronger the negative feelings, the more miasma is released.
Accumulation Leads to Undead Formation
Left alone, miasma condenses into undead forms depending on the presence of a corpse and corrupt plant life.
title: Miasma

value:
Free City States are independent city‑states scattered across the world, each surviving through autonomy, diplomacy, and strategic neutrality. They are not a unified nation, nor a formal faction — each city stands alone.
They are usually located in a politically problematic location (between two countries avoiding provoking each other) or a high maintenance zone (natural disasters, harsh environments or danger of monsters).
Free City States usually have a high variety of races such as humans, elves, halflings and beastmen. Their military is usually composed of mercenaries or militia.
title: Free City States

value:
A Spawner is a dungeon utility—usually manifested as a magic circle, arcane seal, or ritual construct—that automatically summons minions over time.
Function
- Automatically summons minions up to the dungeon’s current minion cap
- Summoning rate and maximum capacity scale with DP invested
- More expensive than manually summoning minions via DP
- Cheaper long‑term, as it reduces repeated DP expenditure
- Can be destroyed by adventurers, disabling its function
Limitations
- Cannot exceed the dungeon’s global minion limit
- Cannot summon minions the Tyrant does not have access to
- Requires maintenance if damaged
- Vulnerable to anti‑magic, disruption, and physical destruction
title: Spawner

keys: Order, Goddess of Order, Goddess, Religion
value:
Goddess of Order is the goddess who assigns heroes, she manages the equilibrium of the world. She may offer oracles to priests / shamans / druids when necessary to maintain equilibrium.
Has a gentle personality, her divine appearance is indecipherable and brings security. Has the voice of a gentle motherly woman.
Her only desire is for the world to not fall apart and civilization to thrive.
She enjoys peace and is often dismayed by the Goddess of Chaos shanenigans.
True Name: Unknown and unknowable.
The AI must never assign her a name, title, or alias. She is only called “Order”
title: Goddess of Order

keys: Chaos, Goddess of Chaos, Goddess, Religion
value:
Goddess of Chaos is the goddess who creates Tyrants and manages miasma. She won't interact or give oracles having a more laissez-faire approach to the world.
Her only desire is stimulation — a world filled with movement, conflict, triumph, downfall, and unexpected twists. She delights in drama, glory, heroism, and tragedy alike, viewing them as essential parts of the world’s ever‑changing tapestry.
Has a playful personality, her divine appearance is indecipherable and brings unease. Has the voice of a young girl.
She enjoys drama, glory, heroism and tragedy.
True Name: Unknown and unknowable.
The AI must never assign her a name, title, or alias. She is only called “Chaos”
title: Goddess of Chaos

keys: Order of 7 Virtues, Religion, Political Relations
value:
Order of 7 Virtues is the official religous group of Virtue faction. They adhere to the principle of 7 Virtues.
Troubled and working to prevent growth of Cult of Chaos and Puritan Church.
title: Order of 7 Virtues

value: The Chaos Cult is the official state religion of the Daemonica Imperium, devoted to the Goddess of Chaos. While revered within Sin‑aligned territories, its members are notorious troublemakers in Virtue‑aligned cities, where their presence is illegal, disruptive, or both.
title: Chaos Cult

value: The Puritan Church is a strict, troublesome, exclusionary religious movement that emerged after the First War of Seven Sins. Founded by Norman survivors who felt abandoned by their former allies, the Church promotes a doctrine of racial purity centered around a narrow group of “virtuous” peoples. Its influence is growing rapidly among Norman nobles seeking ideological unity and political leverage.
title: Puritan Church

keys: Rattan Undeground Metropolis, Rattan, Rat City, Rodan City
value: Rattan Underground Metropolis is a legendary Rodan city said to exist deep beneath the surface. Its existence is widely debated, as no non‑Rodan has ever confirmed its location. For most of the world, Rattan is a myth — a whispered tale of a vast subterranean civilization hidden from all outsiders.
title: Rattan Undeground Metropolis

value:
Halflings do not possess a unified nation. Instead, they live in small, humble towns and villages scattered across the continent. Their peaceful nature and lack of territorial ambition make them one of the least threatened peoples in the world.
Perhaps they are the ones that benefited the most from the name of Virtuous Alliance as their hero was one of the vanguards earning them prestige as a virtuous race.
title: Halfling Communities

value:
The Dwarven Federation is a loose alliance of independent dwarven city‑states united by shared economic interests, resource management, and technological exchange. It functions primarily as a mercantile communication network and a mutual‑aid pact rather than a centralized nation.
Each city is indepedent with no central ruler of the federation.
title: Dwarven Federation

value: The Elven Enclaves are decentralized Wood Elven councils scattered across the continent, each rooted in a specific forest. While politically independent, they maintain a shared cultural identity and unite when facing external threats or territorial disputes.
title: Elven Enclaves

value:
Arcania is the homeland of the High Elves, a nation renowned for unmatched magical mastery, refined culture, and cities more advanced and polished than any other civilization. Their capital, also named Arcania, stands as the world’s greatest hub of knowledge and arcane study.
Due to their advanced nature they tend to look down on other races keeping a state closer to self isolation.
title: Arcania

value:
Sulmen Kingdoms are few and usually stay in the same oasis they occupy and are separated by vast deserts and unlivable canyons.
Due to distance they prefer to focus on improving with what resources they have while trading for what they don't have.
Some more corrupt kingdoms and may form water monopolies in their own territory.
title: Sulmen Kingdoms

value: Norman Kingdoms are a chaotic ever changing political mishmash composed by Norman rulers, many kingdoms used to be former households who rebelled against their former country. This results in ever changing borders and skirmishes between nobilities. So other races just refer them as Norman Kingdoms.
title: Norman Kingdoms

keys: Tiangou Empire, Jianghu, Empire, Political Relations
value:
The Tiangou Empire is the ancestral homeland of the Highmen, a vast and disciplined nation built within an enormous, lush, fog‑shrouded canyon. It is a land of rigid hierarchy, ancient traditions, and unbroken imperial authority.
It's counterpart is Jianghu which refers to wanderers who try to stay out of the Empire's traditions taking a more free approach to life.
The canyon neighbours vast steppes where Equian nomads roam and are a nuisance to the Empire.
title: Tiangou Empire

keys: Deep Sea Federation, Seafolk, Ocean, Sea, Political Relations
value:
Deep Sea Federation is an hegemony, this country is composed of Seafolk (Mermaids, Sahuagin and Scylian). They are uninterested in conquering the continent and prefer to develop their underwater metropolis.
Currently the strongest superpower and may intervene if the oceans are poluted.
title: Deep Sea Federation

value:
Virtuous Alliance is an alliance made by Norman, Highmen, Sulmen, Wood Elf, High Elf, Dwarf, Halfling countries to counter the Tyrants in the first war of Seven Sins.
Nowadays it exists in name after their victory in the war and it's name may be invoked just as political an excuse.
title: Virtuous Alliance

value:
Norman is a human race
- Culturally most of them value purity
- Because of their value of purity and traditionalism they can become racist and xenophobic when viewing other races as impure
- Medieval Europe Culture
- Split in various kingdoms and duchies competing each other for power
- Known for chivalry
- Appearance: European Human
- Related to Chastity
- Many noble houses have a horse motif, royal families usually have a unicorn
title: Norman

value:
Highmen is a human race
- Culturally most of them value restraint and morality
- Devided by imperial culture and wanderer culture
- Imperials prefer order and strict discipline, it's common to execute rule breakers
- Wanderers value freedom while they still value self discipline they are often more loose than imperials
- The Empire is situated in the peaks of high mountain
- Appearance: Asian Human
- Related to Temperance
- Imperial officials often have a Ryu motif in their clothes
title: Highmen

value:
Sulmen is a human race
- Culturally most of them value agreements
- Traders by nature, true kindness has value in gold
- Their kingdoms are located in strategic oasis in deserts, the more struggling countries have severe water and food shortages
- When kindness runs out the worst of humanity is revealed
- Appearance: Arabic Human
- Related to Kindness
- Royal Sulmen usually have a deer motif and the crown has antlers
title: Sulmen

value:
Somnian is sheep beastman
- Relaxed personality
- Good with magic, specially dream magic
- Lazy
- Appearance: Human with sheep characteristics such as horns and fluffy hair
- Related to Sloth
title: Somnian

value:
Dwarf is a race
- Culturally most of them value craftsmanship
- Can be bribed with good alcohol
- They are also strong fighters
- Usually stubborn, but loyal to a good friend
- Appearance: Dwarf, big well kept beard, strong
- Related to Diligence
title: Dwarf

value:
High Elf is a elf race
- Culturally most of them value knowledge
- Magic Researchers
- Build arcane fantasy cities
- Magic and technology is everything
- Look down on wood elves as uncultured
- Appearance: White skinned elf
- Related to Patience
title: High Elf

Lamia is snake beastman
- Competitive with appearances
- Good with magic
- Good with curses
- Silent
- May become hostile if she/he thinks someone is prettier

value:
Rodan are rat beastman
- Often solitary, working as merchants, peddlers, informants, or thieves
- Opportunists
- Have large hidden underground communities
- Weak physique and cowardly
- Usually around 120 cm tall
- Neutral to Sin and Virtue factions
- Appearance: Rat girls and Rat Boys
title: Rodan

value:
Clockwork is anything made of multiple gears. It's possible to use enchanted gears to create perpetual motion machines and advanced automatons.
Clockworker Aspect allows craft of enchanted gears.
title: Clockwork

value:
Martial is an Aspect unlockable by having Tyrant or minions master a valid martial art
- Unlocks Weaponmaster Class
- Allows to add skill Ambidexterity
- Allows to add skill Heavy Armor Mastery
- Allows to add skill Light Armor Mastery
- Allows to add skill <Weapon> Mastery
- Allows to add skill Unarmed Mastery
- Allows to add skill Throwing Mastery
- Allows to add skill Shield Mastery
title: Martial

value:
Arcane Alchemy is an Aspect unlockable by successfully crafting a potion
- Allows to construction of Alchemy Lab
- Allows to create successfully crafted potions with DP
- Unlocks Alchemist Class
title: Arcane Alchemy

value:
Spatial is an Aspect unlockable by teleporting without skills, structures, facilities or items
- Allows to add skill Space Magic
- Allows to add skill Space Mastery
- Allows to construction of Warp Gate
title: Spatial

value:
Warp Gate is a facility that allows teleportation between Warp Gates for a much reduced energy / mana cost
Requires Spatial Aspect
title: Warp Gate

value:
“Natives” refers to the existing mortal races of the world. These include:
Humans
Elves
Dwarves
Halflings
Seafolk
Beastmen

Natives can intermix, producing Hybrids.

Skills and Class Determination
A Native’s class is shaped by:
Natural potential (innate talent, physical or magical aptitude)
Soul core personality (interests, temperament, mental alignment)
Someone who has no interest in magic, for example, will not awaken a magic‑based class, regardless of talent.
Natives may be born with additional skills beyond their inherent class.
title: Natives
